normalized_question_text: For the three-bus power system shown in the figure, the trip signals to the circuit breakers \( B_1 \) to \( B_9 \) are provided by overcurrent relays \( R_1 \) to \( R_9 \), respectively, some of which have directional properties also. The necessary condition for the system to be protected for a short circuit fault at any part of the system between bus 1 and the \( R - L \) loads with isolation of a minimum portion of the network using a minimum number of directional relays is:
normalized_answer_text: \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards bus 2

normalized_choice_map.A: \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards bus 2
normalized_choice_map.B: \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards bus 2 and \( R_7 \) is directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards bus 3
normalized_choice_map.C: \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards Line 1 and Line 2, respectively, \( R_7 \) is a directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards Line 3 and \( R_5 \) is directional overcurrent relay blocking faults towards bus 2
normalized_choice_map.D: \( R_3 \) and \( R_4 \) are directional overcurrent relays blocking faults towards Line 1 and Line 2, respectively.

normalization_notes: Question explicitly depends on the figure for relay and line topology.
Kept original mathematical notation and option wording unchanged.
force_requires_image=true is consistent with the image-dependent structure of the problem.
